desc: Siquijor is a compact Philippine island known for waterfalls, reef snorkeling, and a laid-back scooter-based travel rhythm.
hls: Easy island loop with beaches and inland waterfalls
Strong snorkeling and diving near marine sanctuaries
Less developed vibe than major Visayas resort islands
Good fit for 3-5 night slow stays

todo.name: Cambugahay Falls morning
todo.desc: Arrive early for quieter pools and rope-swing spots.

todo.name: Tubod Marine Sanctuary snorkel
todo.desc: Do a reef session with current-aware local guides.

todo.name: Paliton Beach sunset
todo.desc: Plan late-afternoon swimming and sunset viewing.

todo.name: Lazi church and convent
todo.desc: Visit one of the Philippines' oldest stone church complexes.

todo.name: Island scooter loop
todo.desc: Circle the island with stops at viewpoints and cafés.

todo.name: Mt. Bandilaan forest reserve
todo.desc: Take a short inland hike for cooler forest conditions.

wifi.note: Improving in beachfront stays, but outages still occur during storms.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland routes typically connect via Manila, Cebu, Singapore, or Doha, then combine domestic flight and ferry; Amihan dry season (Dec-May) brings stronger demand and pricing.
prac.currency: Philippines local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Ferry schedules are weather-sensitive, especially during typhoon-active months.
Island roads are narrow and can be slick after rain—ride scooters cautiously at night.
ATMs are limited and sometimes offline; carry backup cash before weekends.

sweet: Dec-May for calmer seas and better ferry reliability.
